---
title: "grub &gt; invalid root specified"
date: 2005-07-27
forum: Installation &amp; Upgrades
---

### Post by ouphe on 2005-07-27
Hi there!

I cannot boot into Ubuntu. A thing called LVM says i did not specify the correct root. I'm overlooking something, but i cannot find out what it is.

menu.lst:
# grub.conf generated by anaconda
#
# Note that you do not have to rerun grub after making changes to this file
# NOTICE:  You have a /boot partition.  This means that
#          all kernel and initrd paths are relative to /boot/, eg.
#          root (hd1,0)
#          kernel /vmlinuz-version ro root=/dev/VolGroup00/LogVol00
#          initrd /initrd-version.img
#boot=/dev/hda
default=2
timeout=5
splashimage=(hd1,0)/grub/splash.xpm.gz
hiddenmenu

title Fedora Core (2.6.11-1.1369_FC4)
        root (hd1,0)
        kernel /vmlinuz-2.6.11-1.1369_FC4 ro root=/dev/VolGroup00/LogVol00 rhgb quiet
        initrd /initrd-2.6.11-1.1369_FC4.img

title Ubuntu 5.04
        root (hd2,1)
        kernel /vmlinuz root=/dev/hdc2

title Windows XP
        rootnoverify (hd0,0)
        chainloader +1


disk info:
Device Boot      Start         End      Blocks   Id  System
/dev/hdc1               1       13753   110470941    7  HPFS/NTFS
/dev/hdc2   *       13754       14554     6434032+  83  Linux
/dev/hdc3           14555       14593      313267+   5  Extended
/dev/hdc5           14555       14593      313236   82  Linux swap / Solaris

XP and Fedora are fine. Expert setup was done with automatic partitioning. The boot arguments were taken from the installation info. I did not install a boot loader. Please share your thoughts :)

---

### Post by Xian on 2005-07-27
Is your Ubuntu partition not using an initrd.img file to boot?

---

### Post by ouphe on 2005-07-27
Works like a charm again! It should have been:

root            (hd2,1)
kernel          /boot/vmlinuz-2.6.10-5-386 root=/dev/hdc2 ro quiet splash

thanks Xian! So offcourse:
initrd          /boot/initrd.img-2.6.10-5-386

I thought this file was only necessary for the splash screen

---

### Post by Xian on 2005-07-27
I'm surprised you don't have a /vmlinuz symlink.
That should be there by default.

There should also be one for /initrd.img
This is very helpful when updating kernels.
No need to continually update your menu.lst.

Maybe it points to the wrong file....

---
